title: init_swtpm uses bare i32 error return instead of typed error
description: wolftpm_sys::swtpm::init_swtpm returns Result<Box<WOLFTPM2_DEV>, i32> with i32 documented as 'wolfTPM return code'. This is a C-style error code in a Rust Result. Library callers (wolftpm, wolftpm-tss) cannot inspect the error variant or distinguish wolfTPM codes from libc errors (-1 from setenv). A typed error enum (e.g. SwtpmInitError { Libc(i32), WolfTpm(i32) }) would let callers branch on the failure cause and display it correctly. Current callers map it to their own types via pattern match on the naked i32.
